# Transcrição: 26. Trait 25 - Great Salespeople are not satisfied with awards and paychecks.

- **Fonte:** 26. Trait 25 - Great Salespeople are not satisfied with awards and paychecks.
- **Duração:** 00:58
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 00:58]

The great salespeople are not satisfied with just awards and paychecks. Okay? Oh good, give me a little trophy. What am I going to do with that? Collect 26 of them? Right. Okay, paychecks. Same thing. They just go to the bank. Look, that will only produce so much motivation before you're like, okay, I got too many trophies. I got too many checks. I got a bunch of money in the bank. The great salespeople know they must be driven by something other than just money and awards and rewards. They're motivated. These people are guided. They have some all kind of, some kind of all-in inspiration, something driving them from the inside that keeps them showing up long after others think they need to be involved in the game. The greats are striving to satisfy something inside them, something deep, something at the core that all human beings have. If you don't think you have it, that's not true. You just haven't found it. The greats have found it and the greats utilize it every day to fulfill their purpose.
